title: Amoria repens
taxonomy: Amoria repens is classified as a species of saltwater clam, specifically within the family Veneridae. This classification is based on its shell characteristics and its habitat in marine environments. Amoria repens is a type of bivalve mollusk, which is a broad group of marine animals that also includes oysters and mussels. The family Veneridae includes a large number of species of saltwater clams, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical waters. Amoria repens is one of the species within this family that is known for its distinctive shell shape and size.

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
